meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Build your first SaaS with this student-focused starter kit. Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch real-world apps.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by building a functional SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform abstract coding skills into a verifiable product. This project proves you can ship, deploy, and maintain software, instantly separating you from other junior applicants.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank GitHub contributions graph, feeling the weight of the 'no experience' label. Every job posting demands a portfolio you don't have, creating a cycle of frustration that keeps you on the sidelines. The industry doesn't want another generic to-do app; they want to see that you can navigate the complexities of a real, production-ready SaaS environment.

Today, we break that cycle. By treating your first project as a professional SaaS starter kit, you stop being a student and start being a builder. Using industry-grade tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, we will compress months of trial-and-error into a rapid, high-impact development cycle that turns your laptop into a launchpad.

steps.title: Define Architecture with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish the foundational database schema and authentication flow. This sets the blueprint for a scalable SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable SaaS architecture for a subscription-based task manager including Prisma schema for PostgreSQL and NextAuth configuration.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Initialize your project environment ensuring strict TypeScript standards and environment variable management for production readiness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI components optimized for a SaaS dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "setup project structure with tailwind and shadcn"

steps.title: Implement Stripe Subscription Logic
steps.description: Integrate payments to demonstrate you understand the business side of SaaS, which is a massive differentiator for recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler and subscription checkout flow for a Next.js 14 app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "integrate stripe checkout and webhook handling"

steps.title: Build the Protected Dashboard
steps.description: Create a user-specific dashboard that demonstrates state management and secure API route protection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a protected user dashboard component that fetches data based on session ID and displays it in a responsive grid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "create secure dashboard route with user data fetching"

steps.title: Automate Deployment and CI/CD
steps.description: Configure your project to deploy automatically on commit, showing you understand professional DevOps workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs linting, testing, and deploys to Vercel upon push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate github actions pipeline for automatic deployment"

pro_tips: Always include a 'README.md' that explains your design decisions; recruiters care more about your thought process than the code itself.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first: get a user from registration to payment in under 20 clicks to ensure the core value prop works.
Use mock data early to build the UI, then swap in the real API calls once the layout is polished.
Add a 'Status' page or 'Changelog' to your app; it shows you understand how to communicate updates to real users.
Document your bugs in the code comments; it proves you can troubleshoot and reflect on your technical challenges.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, add one unique feature that solves a specific problem you personally face, then push the code to GitHub and pin it to your profile.
content.conversion_block: You now have a blueprint to transform your career from 'student' to 'software engineer'. Stop waiting for permission to build and start shipping products that actually work. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
